designSystem.styleGuidelines:
## Brand & Style

The design system embodies "Rustic High-Fashion," a sophisticated juxtaposition of raw, organic botanical beauty and the disciplined structure of high-end editorial magazines. It targets a discerning clientele who appreciates the artistry of floristry as much as the luxury of the experience.

The visual language leans into **High-Contrast Minimalism** mixed with **Editorial Layouts**. Expect heavy whitespace, intentional asymmetry, and overlapping elements that mimic the natural layering of a bouquet. The emotional response should be one of "Quiet Luxury"—grounded, authentic, yet unmistakably premium. All elements must maintain sharp, architectural edges to preserve a sense of deliberate craftsmanship and modern elegance.

## Layout & Spacing

This design system utilizes a **Fixed Grid** for desktop and a **Fluid Grid** for mobile. 

- **Grid:** A 12-column grid with wide 32px gutters to prevent content from feeling crowded. 
- **Whitespace:** Use whitespace as a functional element. Sections should be separated by large vertical gaps (80px to 120px) to allow the "floating leaf" motifs and photography to breathe.
- **Composition:** Encourage "Editorial Overlaps" where images may break the grid or overlap with text blocks. Use hairline borders (1px) in Stem Green or Morning Mist to define zones without adding visual weight.

## Elevation & Depth

To maintain the high-fashion editorial aesthetic, avoid traditional shadows. Depth is achieved through **Tonal Layering** and **Structural Overlaps**.

- **Tiers:** Use the contrast between Petal Cream (Level 1) and Morning Mist (Level 2) to signify depth.
- **Hairline Outlines:** Use 1px solid borders in Stem Green (#2D3A28) at 20-30% opacity to define cards and containers.
- **Floating Motifs:** The "floating leaf" visual rhythm is created by placing transparent PNG botanical assets at different z-indices, occasionally overlapping text or image borders to create a 3D collage effect.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Rectangular with a 1px border. Default state: Stem Green text on transparent background. Hover state: Stem Green background with Petal Cream text. Transition should be instant or very fast (150ms) to feel "sharp."
- **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only (1px Stem Green). Labels should use the uppercase "label-md" typography style floating above the line.
- **Cards:** No shadows. Defined by a 1px "Morning Mist" border or a slight "Morning Mist" background fill. Images within cards should always be top-aligned and full-width.
- **Chips/Tags:** Small rectangular boxes with 1px borders. Use "label-md" typography.
- **Signature Motif:** A "Floating Leaf" component—a decorative asset that can be placed at the edge of containers, providing an organic break to the rigid grid.
- **Image Treatments:** Use "Image-on-Image" stacks where a smaller, detail-oriented photo overlaps the corner of a larger hero image.

  gutter: 32px
  margin-desktop: 64px
  margin-mobile: 24px
---

## Brand & Style

The design system embodies "Rustic High-Fashion," a sophisticated juxtaposition of raw, organic botanical beauty and the disciplined structure of high-end editorial magazines. It targets a discerning clientele who appreciates the artistry of floristry as much as the luxury of the experience.

The visual language leans into **High-Contrast Minimalism** mixed with **Editorial Layouts**. Expect heavy whitespace, intentional asymmetry, and overlapping elements that mimic the natural layering of a bouquet. The emotional response should be one of "Quiet Luxury"—grounded, authentic, yet unmistakably premium. All elements must maintain sharp, architectural edges to preserve a sense of deliberate craftsmanship and modern elegance.

## Colors

The palette is rooted in the "Stem Green" primary, providing a deep, sophisticated anchor. "Petal Cream" serves as the main background surface to evoke the texture of fine stationery, while "Morning Mist" is used for subtle UI partitioning and secondary surfaces.

"Pressed Rose" and "Wildflower Gold" are used sparingly for highlights, calls to action, and decorative flourishes. Contrast is key; maintain high legibility by using Stem Green for most text against Petal Cream surfaces. Avoid pure blacks; the deep green serves as the "technical black" of the system.

## Typography

The typography strategy relies on the tension between the high-contrast Serifs and the spaced-out Sans-Serifs. 

- **Headlines:** Use Playfair Display for all major headings. It should feel authoritative and graceful. Use "Display" sizes for hero sections with tight letter spacing.
- **Body:** Montserrat is utilized for readability. Crucially, apply generous letter spacing (tracking) to all body text to enhance the airy, editorial feel.
- **Labels:** Use uppercase Montserrat with extra tracking (15%+) for small UI labels, buttons, and navigation items to evoke the look of high-fashion mastheads.

## Layout & Spacing

This design system utilizes a **Fixed Grid** for desktop and a **Fluid Grid** for mobile. 

- **Grid:** A 12-column grid with wide 32px gutters to prevent content from feeling crowded. 
- **Whitespace:** Use whitespace as a functional element. Sections should be separated by large vertical gaps (80px to 120px) to allow the "floating leaf" motifs and photography to breathe.
- **Composition:** Encourage "Editorial Overlaps" where images may break the grid or overlap with text blocks. Use hairline borders (1px) in Stem Green or Morning Mist to define zones without adding visual weight.

## Elevation & Depth

To maintain the high-fashion editorial aesthetic, avoid traditional shadows. Depth is achieved through **Tonal Layering** and **Structural Overlaps**.

- **Tiers:** Use the contrast between Petal Cream (Level 1) and Morning Mist (Level 2) to signify depth.
- **Hairline Outlines:** Use 1px solid borders in Stem Green (#2D3A28) at 20-30% opacity to define cards and containers.
- **Floating Motifs:** The "floating leaf" visual rhythm is created by placing transparent PNG botanical assets at different z-indices, occasionally overlapping text or image borders to create a 3D collage effect.

## Shapes

The shape language is strictly **Architectural and Sharp**. 

- **Corners:** No rounded corners are permitted. All buttons, input fields, image containers, and cards must have a 0px border-radius. 
- **Dividers:** Use horizontal and vertical hairline strokes (1px) to separate content. These strokes should feel like the "grid lines" of a layout sheet.
- **Masking:** Use sharp rectangular masks for all photography.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Rectangular with a 1px border. Default state: Stem Green text on transparent background. Hover state: Stem Green background with Petal Cream text. Transition should be instant or very fast (150ms) to feel "sharp."
- **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only (1px Stem Green). Labels should use the uppercase "label-md" typography style floating above the line.
- **Cards:** No shadows. Defined by a 1px "Morning Mist" border or a slight "Morning Mist" background fill. Images within cards should always be top-aligned and full-width.
- **Chips/Tags:** Small rectangular boxes with 1px borders. Use "label-md" typography.
- **Signature Motif:** A "Floating Leaf" component—a decorative asset that can be placed at the edge of containers, providing an organic break to the rigid grid.
- **Image Treatments:** Use "Image-on-Image" stacks where a smaller, detail-oriented photo overlaps the corner of a larger hero image.

screens.name: projects/10325434914065539101/screens/389664e9928c4db88b01cdea1020ca53
screens.prompt:
Build a complete DESKTOP landing page for Rock Hill Flower Market Shoppe. Visual Style: "Rustic High-Fashion". Sophisticated serif-led typographic hierarchy (Playfair Display), generous whitespace, and a custom color palette (Stem Green, Petal Cream, Pressed Rose). Use an editorial, asymmetric layout with overlapping elements and a "floating leaf" motif. Sections to include: 1. Header: Top bar with phone number (803) 250-6479 and semantic navigation. 2. Hero: Asymmetric layout with large-scale botanical imagery ({{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_6}}) and elegant serif typography. 3. Credibility: A section highlighting the brand's unique artistry and local Rock Hill roots. 4. Services/Offerings: Showcase bespoke bouquets, event artistry, and sympathy designs with an editorial grid. Use {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_4}}. 5. About: A narrative section on the philosophy of floral design. Use {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_7}} for the studio interior. 6. Visit: Section with contact details (1143 North Anderson Road, Rock Hill, SC 29730) and a map slot:
. 7. Contact Form: A minimalist, high-end inquiry form with validation UI states. 8. Footer: Complete semantic footer with social links and contact info (info@rhflowermarketshoppe.com). Technical requirements: Responsive, smooth scroll, CSS Custom Properties for colors, descriptive alt tags, and no lorem ipsum. Ensure it feels like a bespoke high-fashion editorial.

screens.title: Rock Hill Flower Market Shoppe - Home

outputComponents.text:
I have completed the high-fidelity desktop landing page for **Rock Hill Flower Market Shoppe**, fully realizing the "Rustic High-Fashion" editorial identity.

### Design Implementation Highlights:
*   **Editorial Hero & Layout**: The design uses an asymmetric grid and overlapping elements to break the "template" feel, creating a sophisticated digital presence that mirrors the organic precision of your floral work.
*   **Visual Identity**: I've integrated the custom color palette (Stem Green, Petal Cream, Pressed Rose) and the "floating leaf" motif throughout, using thin hairline borders for an architectural, modern touch.
*   **Production-Ready Sections**: 
    *   An elegant **Hero** featuring high-end botanical imagery.
    *   A **Services** grid for bespoke orders and events.
    *   A **Visit** section with a dedicated map slot and studio hours.
    *   A minimalist **Inquiry Form** designed for high-end conversion.
*   **Refined Typography**: Large-scale Playfair Display headings paired with generous whitespace establish immediate premium credibility.

The code is built with semantic HTML, responsive CSS using custom properties, and includes a mobile-ready navigation system.

Would you like to see how this translates to a mobile-specific variant, or shall we explore some subtle scroll-triggered motion effects for the botanical elements?

outputComponents.suggestion: Add motion effects to the hero
